title: advance() expect() is a justified programming-contract assertion
description: In lexer.rs, advance() calls chars.next().expect(). Every call site first verifies chars.peek().is_some() before calling advance(). The expect() is therefore an assertion that a logic invariant holds, not a user-input error. It panics only if the lexer has a bug (calling advance on an empty iterator), not on malformed Sieve scripts. A reviewer might suggest returning Option<char> or using unwrap_or, but those would obscure a real logic bug. The expect() with a descriptive message is the correct Rust pattern here.
